name: Smoked Leg of Lamb
recipeIngredient: 2 1/2 c Hickory chips; soaked for 1 hour & drained
recipeInstructions:
1. Trim excess fat from lamb.
2. Cut 1/2-inch incisions 2 inches apart over the surface of lamb; insert slivers of garlic into incisions.
3. Rub lamb with salt and pepper.
4. Combine vermouth, olive oil, bay leaf, thyme, and rosemary in a small bowl; work the mixture into lamb. Lamb can be refrigerated at this point, tightly wrapped in plastic, up to 24 hours. Bring to room temperature before smoking.
5. Set up smoker using 8 pounds charcoal briquets and 2-1/2 cups soaked and drained hickory chips. Add 5 quarts water to the water pan.
6. Place lamb on the upper rack of the smoker; smoke until an instant-read thermometer inserted into the thickest part of meat away from bone registers 150°F (medium) or 160°F (well done), 4 to 6 hours.
7. Serve hot or at room temperature.
